{"id":298,"date":"2026-06-27T11:31:05","date_gmt":"2026-06-27T11:31:05","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/?post_type=trail&p=298"},"modified":"2026-07-23T11:05:55","modified_gmt":"2026-07-23T11:05:55","slug":"cabanbanan-community-trail-loop","status":"publish","type":"trail","link":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/trails\/cabanbanan-community-trail-loop\/","title":{"rendered":"Cabanbanan Community Trail Loop | Camp 8, Toledo City | 2026 GPX"},"content":{"rendered":"\n

The Cabanbanan Community Trail Loop is a community hike in Toledo City that passes through rolling farmland, quiet rural roads, and mountain viewpoints. Although the route is generally easy to follow, its continuous ascents and descents over approximately 8.34 km require moderate physical effort and are better suited to hikers with some basic hiking experience.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
MILD<\/div>View others \u2192<\/a><\/div>
\n
\ud83d\udcc5 Documented<\/span>June 2026<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udccf Distance<\/span>8.34 km (Loop)<\/strong><\/div>
\u26f0 Elevation Gain<\/span>335 m<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udd52 Duration<\/span>2-3 hrs<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 1 liter<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Limited<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>25% trail, 75% road<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Wikiloc<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began near Camp 7 National High School. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, with no parking fee observed during the hike. The jump-off was reached via a combination of concrete and dirt roads. Private vehicles could access the area, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

The documented parking area was approximately 2 km before the junction leading to Canapnapan 2.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Mild<\/h3>
\u2713 Mostly easy-to-follow trail<\/div>
\u2713 Basic hiking experience recommended<\/div>
\u2713 Moderate physical effort required<\/div>
\u2713 Moderate route commitment<\/div>
\u2713 Sustained moderate ascent or descent<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> farmland<\/a> \u2022<\/span> ridge<\/a> \u2022<\/span> roads<\/a> \u2022<\/span> rolling hills<\/a> \u2022<\/span> viewpoint<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The route follows a mostly straightforward community trail through Camp 7, Canapnapan 2, Canapnapan 3, Cabanbanan, and Upper Camp 8. The trail consists of steady climbs and descents across open farmland and rural landscapes, with frequent encounters with local residents.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Several sections provide mountain viewpoints along the route. The trail is non-technical, with the primary challenge coming from the repeated elevation changes rather than difficult terrain.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Hiking the loop in reverse results in a long, continuous climb beginning from the road junction toward Cabanbanan.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but was observed to become very muddy after rain. Shade coverage was limited, and several stores were available near local communities.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Mobile signal was strong near the start and intermittent in some sections of the route. At least 1 liter of water was recommended. Many people were encountered during the hike, while insect activity was minimal.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

This is a community trail with homes scattered throughout the route and is not considered remote. The local residents, especially the children encountered during the hike, were observed to be friendly and helpful.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

A hiking stick or trekking pole is recommended for hikers who are uncomfortable around dogs, as several dogs may be encountered along the route.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
